title: HEAT Letter: Zero-parameter redshift evolution of late-type galaxy sizes from a0(z) proportional to H(z)
description: Companion code and LaTeX source for the HEAT (Hubble-Emergent Acceleration Theory) letter. HEAT identifies the MOND acceleration scale with the FRW apparent-horizon surface gravity divided by 2 pi, a0(z) = c H(z) / (2 pi), and predicts the late-type size-redshift relation R(z)/R_0 = (1+z)^{-3/4} [Om_m(z)/Om_m(0)]^{1/4} at fixed baryonic mass. The headline observable is invariant under the proportionality constant: only the scaling a0 proportional to H(z) enters, while the 1/(2 pi) is anchored to SPARC at z=0. This release reproduces the seven figures of the letter: (i) the closed-form R(z)/R_0 normalisation curve with its asymptote Om_m^{-1/4} and the MMW98 disk-formation baseline as reference; (ii) the stellar-mass-normalised compactification R_obs/R_0 versus redshift, with a binned MUSE-DARK MHUDF overlay from Ciocan+2026 Paper I; (iii) the ALMA z ~ 4.5 joint kinematic test (N=4); (iv) the BTFR zero-point evolution from SPARC + cosmic-noon + ALMA, including the Jeanneau+2026 lensed-bTFR null at z~1 as the principal extant challenge; (v) the SPARC 171-galaxy local-anchor chi^2 comparison with constant-a0 MOND (p=0.46); (vi) the quantitative mass-selection robustness test ruling out one-parameter M_b(z) mimics of R(z); and (vii) the direct a0(z) and DM-density evolution test against the MUSE-DARK trilogy (Ciocan+2026 Papers I & III, Vărașteanu+2025). The intermediate-z RAR-derived a0(z) is consistent with the HEAT shape (free-K chi^2 = 7.2 for 5 dof) and matches HEAT's z=0 normalisation in the MOND framework to within 1 percent; the Paper I halo-density rise rho_s ~ (1+z)^{0.54+/-0.31} agrees with HEAT's predicted log_10[H(z)/H_0] scaling within 0.7 sigma; the Paper II lensed-bTFR null at z~1 sits ~4 sigma below the HEAT prediction and is treated as a falsification target. The hypothesis stands or falls on percent-level R(z)/R_0 over 0 lesssim z lesssim 3 from Euclid/Roman, plus the direct HI-based bTFR test at z~1. Included are the analysis scripts, the Rotmod_LTG SPARC rotation-curve catalogue, the heat_data/ public catalogues from the MUSE-DARK trilogy, the paper LaTeX source (publication/paper_heat_letter.tex), and the pre-generated figures under heat_output/.
